raw_text: janot vilaró jolis barcelona, 02 july 2003 personal email address: janot.vilaro@gmail.com student email address: janot.vilaro@estudiantat.upc.edu personal telephone number: +34 688 861 589 internships and research activity (15/01/25-present) full-time junior researcher on quantum optics for quantum information. bachelor's thesis on quantum optics at université paris-saclay centre de nanosciences et de nanotechnologie (c2n) with the toniq team, directed by dr. kamel bencheikh. what is being done: theoretical calculations and computer simulations to assess the viability of performing the so-called quantum non-destructive measurements (qnd) on a millimeter-scale chip based on non-linear waveguides integrated on thin film lithium niobate on insulator platform, which hold great promise for potential quantum information applications. personal outcomes: besides the specific details of the project i am currently working on, i am strengthening notably my communication skills thanks to the continuous results-discussions with the team. moreover, i get the opportunity to exploit my proactivity and problem- solving skills by repeatedly having to come up with new ideas and solutions to tackle the problem in hand. (10/23- 12/24) part-time junior researcher on machine learning algorithms for crystal structure prediction. internship with the quantum and energy materials research modelling group directed by dr. claudio cazorla silva. what was done: the developement of a new code for the prediction of crystal structures of novel materials that is based on machine learning and genetic algorithms. this crystal structure prediction code is being used to theoretically investigate a family of relatively unknown materials that hold great promise for photovoltaic applications. additionally, vasp calculations were performed on high performance computing facilities (hpc) like marenostrum5, to study the aforementioned materials. personal outcomes: during this first work experience i established a strong relation with the team thanks to the group meetings held every two weeks. this also reinforced my presentation skills, helping me organize my work and present it in an intuitive way for the rest of the team-members. publications - design and optimization of a paul ion trap. e a project supervised by dr. juan manuel rius that will be published soon on upc commons by dr. pere bruna. - environmental impact of agriculture: quantifying tools and ways to take action. e a class project presented with some peers that will be published soon on upc commons by prof. eva vidal. - pendular waves. e a class project supervised by dr. rafael ramírez ros, published on his website. college studies - (2021-present) bachelors degree in physics engineering at upc, barcelona. (expected graduation mark: 8/10) e this degree provides a sound and broad background in theoretical physics along with a strong foundation on telecommunication engineering-related topics. - (2022) seminar about finance and engineering for investment, at upc, barcelona. programming skills - proficient in: matlab and numerical optimization // python. - experienced in: fortran // c // arduino. - familiar with: openmp. - operating systems: familiar with linux-based enviroments and terminal interface. - document processing: latex // excel, word and other office productivity tools. selection of some projects some of them are published in my github. - building a neural netwrok from scratch for handwritten character recognition: python, ai, neural-networks. - design and optimization of a paul ion trap: 3d meshes, delaunays method, numerical electromagnetics. - crystal structure prediction and density functional theory energy relaxations for cu-antiperovskites: high performance computing facilities, quantum and energy materials. - design and electronics of an automated awning: arduino, circuit theory. language skills catalan as mother tongue. spanish as mother tongue. english: full professional proficiency. e certificate in advanced english c1 cambridge assessment. e toefl ibt (108/120). french: full professional proficiency. e baccalauréat, b2 french certificate. high school studies (2019-2021) spanish and french baccalaureate in physics/ chemistry, and computer science, at institut montserrat, barcelona. e my grade was 9,87/10, with mention trés bien avec les félicitations du jury and honors. (2018-2019) studied the 10th year abroad in oakland technical high school, california, usa. e mentions obtained while in the us: o california legislature assembly certificate of recognition for academic excellence. (4.0 gpa) o oakland unified school district honor roll award for exemplary academic achievement. (4.0 gpa) (2014-2018) studied middle school in la salle bonanova, barcelona.
